text: Alarm clocks are just devices that force you to experience the worst possible way to wake up—violated by an angry robot at an ungodly hour. I mean, imagine if alarm clocks were honest: they'd come with a warning label that says "This device will make you hate mornings more than you already do." The snooze button is basically the alarm clock industry's way of saying "We know you're going to lose this battle, so here's five more minutes of false hope." And the real tragedy? We actually pay money for this torture. You could wake up naturally at 6 AM on a weekend and feel like garbage, but somehow when your alarm screams at you at 6 AM on a workday, it's even worse. It's like the alarm clock knows when you actually want to sleep and times its assault accordingly.
